label: Abstinence
description: The virtue moderating food by reason.

label: Adam's First Sin
description: The tract-local doctrinal case of the first sin of the first man, treated under pride without collapsing into the generic vice node.

label: Adultery
description: Adultery as treated within the species of lust in q.154.

label: Anger
description: Passion of the irascible appetite concerning injury and vindication.

label: Anger (Vice-Level)
description: Anger insofar as it becomes sinful by setting aside the order of reason.

label: Beauty / Comeliness of Virtue
description: The honorable beauty or comeliness especially associated with temperance and honesty.

label: Bodily Defects (Punishments of the First Sin)
description: Bodily corruption, sickness, and defect as punishments following the loss of original integrity.

label: Boorishness / Rudeness
description: The vice of deficient playfulness that makes a person burdensome to others in ordinary intercourse.

label: Cardinal Virtue
description: Principal moral virtue around which others are ordered.

label: Chastity
description: The virtue by which reason chastises concupiscence in venereal pleasures.

label: Clemency
description: The virtue that mitigates punishment under the rule of reason.

label: Continence
description: Reason's resistance to vehement evil desires without the full ease of perfect temperance.

label: Cruelty
description: The vice opposed to clemency by exceeding due severity in punishment.

label: Curiosity
description: Disordered pursuit of knowledge or inquiry, opposed to studiousness in this temperance tract.

label: Death (Punishment of the First Sin)
description: Death as the common punishment flowing from the first sin.

label: Decalogue
description: The principal set of precepts delivered in the Old Law.

label: Disgrace
description: Disgrace as the feared deformity opposed to temperance and related virtue.

label: Disordered Inquiry
description: The inordinate study or pursuit of knowledge treated under curiosity.

label: Divine Likeness
description: The likeness to God that the first man inordinately coveted beyond due measure.

label: Drink
description: Drink as matter for sobriety and drunkenness in the temperance tract.

label: Drunkenness
description: Immoderate use of wine or intoxicating drink resulting in loss of reason.

label: Wittiness / Eutrapelia
description: The virtue governing games and playful actions according to reason as part of external modesty.

label: Excessive Adornment
description: Immoderate attention to outward apparel seeking glory, pleasure, or excessive solicitude.

label: Excessive Play
description: The vice of going beyond reason in playful words or deeds.

label: Modesty in Outward Movements
description: The tract-local species of modesty governing outward bodily movements and serious external behavior.

label: Fasting
description: A virtuous act of abstinence undertaken under the rule of reason.

label: Gift of Fear of the Lord
description: Gift of the Holy Spirit producing reverential fear before God.

label: Temptation of the First Parents
description: The tract-local temptation case leading toward Adam's first sin by intellective and sensitive incentives.

label: Food
description: Food as matter for abstinence, fasting, and gluttony within the temperance tract.

label: Gluttony
description: Inordinate desire of eating and drinking opposed to abstinence.

label: God
description: God as the object or term of theological virtue and charity.

label: Honesty (Temperance Tract)
description: Honesty as the honorable comeliness of virtue and an integral part of temperance in this tract.

label: Humility
description: The virtue that restrains a person from aiming above due measure and submits the spirit under God.

label: Incest
description: Incest as treated within the species of lust in q.154.

label: Incontinence
description: Failure to stand by reason under vehement passion, especially in pleasures of touch and secondarily in anger.

label: Insensibility (Against Temperance)
description: Defective refusal of pleasures that nature requires in due measure, opposed to temperance in q.142.

label: Intemperance
description: Vice opposed to temperance.

label: Knowledge
description: Cognitive grasp used here for prophecy and other intellectual acts.

label: Knowledge of Good and Evil
description: The knowledge-related likeness to God especially coveted in the first sin narrative as interpreted in this tract.

label: Loss of Paradise
description: The deprivation of the place and state befitting original innocence after the first sin.

label: Lust
description: Disordered seeking of venereal pleasure against right reason.

label: Meekness
description: The virtue moderating the passion of anger under reason.

label: Modesty (General)
description: The annexed virtue moderating lesser matters where restraint is easier than in temperance proper.

label: Nocturnal Pollution
description: Nocturnal venereal pollution as treated in q.154 a.5.

label: Ordered Inquiry
description: The rightly ordered pursuit of knowledge under reason and due end.

label: Outward Apparel
description: Dress, ornament, and outward attire as matter moderated under exterior modesty.

label: Modesty in Outward Apparel
description: The tract-local exterior-modesty species governing attire, ornament, and dress under reason and fitting custom.

label: Outward Behavior
description: The field of bodily bearing, gesture, and exterior deportment moderated under this part of modesty.

label: Outward Moderation
description: The broader field of lesser matters moderated by modesty in q.160.

label: One's Own Excellence
description: The difficult good or excellence inordinately desired by pride.

label: Parts / Species of Lust
description: The tract-level division of lust into distinct species treated in q.154.

label: Parts of Temperance (General Taxonomy)
description: The tract-level teaching that temperance has integral, subjective, and potential parts.

label: Playful Actions
description: Words or deeds whose immediate end is delight and rest of soul in play.

label: Pleasures of Touch
description: The proper matter of temperance in the restricted sense emphasized in q.141.

label: Precepts of Temperance
description: The tract-local synthesis of precepts directly bearing on temperance itself.

label: Precepts of the Parts of Temperance
description: The tract-local synthesis of precepts touching the annexed virtues and opposed vices of temperance's parts.

label: Pride
description: Inordinate self-exaltation by which a person aims above what is proportionate under the divine rule.

label: Punishment
description: Punishment as the matter clemency mitigates under the rule of reason.

label: Purity (Temperance Tract)
description: Purity insofar as it moderates pleasures incidental to venereal acts in the temperance tract.

label: Rape
description: Rape as treated within the species or grave acts of lust in q.154.

label: Reason
description: Reason as rule and measure of human acts.

label: Rebellion of the Carnal Appetite
description: The disorder whereby the lower appetite rebels against reason as punishment of the first sin.

label: Sacrilege (Lust Tract)
description: Sacrilege insofar as sacred persons or things are violated by venereal sin in q.154.

label: Savagery / Brutality
description: Bestial or savage attack distinct from cruelty properly so called in q.159 a.2.

label: Seduction
description: Seduction as treated among the species or tracted cases of lust in q.154.

label: Venereal / Sexual Pleasure
description: The venereal matter treated by chastity, virginity, lust, and the parts of lust.

label: Shamefacedness
description: The fear of disgrace that stands as an integral part of temperance while falling short of perfect virtue.

label: Simple Fornication
description: The union of an unmarried man and an unmarried woman treated as a species of lust in q.154.

label: Sin
description: Bad human act contrary to due rule and measure.

label: Sobriety
description: The virtue moderating intoxicating drink for the safeguard of reason.

label: Studiousness
description: The virtue moderating the soul's natural desire to know.

label: Temperance
description: The cardinal virtue moderating attractions of pleasure.

label: Truth About Oneself
description: The rule by which humility knows and acknowledges one's proportion and deficiency.

label: Vice Against Nature
description: Venereal acts from which generation cannot follow, treated as a species of lust in q.154.

label: Venereal Touches / Kisses
description: Disordered venereal touching and kissing treated in q.154 a.4.

label: Vice
description: Vice considered as privative disorder contrary to virtue.

label: Virginity
description: The tract's virtue or state of perpetual integrity ordered to freedom from venereal experience.

label: Virtue
description: Generic virtue as good operative perfection.
notes: Do not collapse generic virtue into a specific virtue without explicit passage support.
